title: potentilla anserina var. rolandii
intro: Potentilla anserina var. rolandii, also known as marsh cinquefoil, is a perennial plant in the rose family that grows in wetlands and has a distinctive yellow flower. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of marsh cinquefoil include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and urinary tract infections. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
